data.title: Aker Solutions ASA: Invitation to presentation of third-quarter results for 2025
data.description:
Aker Solutions ASA

October 17, 2025 - Aker Solutions will publish its third-quarter results for 2025 on Friday, October 31st, at 07:00 CET.  

An online presentation will take place on the same day at 09:00 CET. To join the presentation, go to www.akersolutions.com/webcast 

A Q&A session will follow the presentation. Written questions can be submitted through the online solution or sent in advance to preben.orbeck@akersolutions.com from investor relations. 

An opportunity for individual press interviews will follow the presentation. Please direct your interest to hallvard.norum@akersolutions.com from communications.  

The third-quarter presentation will be available on https://www.akersolutions.com and https://newsweb.oslobors.no

ENDS

Preben Ørbeck

investor relations

preben.orbeck@akersolutions.com

+47 470 10 611

Hallvard Norum

media contact

hallvard.norum@akersolutions.com

+47 913 80 820

Aker Solutions delivers integrated solutions, products and services to the global energy industry. We enable low-carbon oil and gas production and develop renewable solutions to meet future energy needs. By combining innovative digital solutions and predictable project execution we accelerate the transition to sustainable energy production. Aker Solutions employs approximately 12,000 people in more than 15 countries.

Visit akersolutions.com and connect with us on Facebook, Instagram, LinkedIn and YouTube.

This press release may include forward-looking information or statements and is subject to our disclaimer, see https://akersolutions.com

This information is subject to the disclosure requirements pursuant to section 5-12 of the Norwegian Securities Trading Act.

data.title: EU Mulls Tying Funds to Pension Reform, Politico Reports, Citing Sources
data.description:
• The European Commission is considering withholding funds from its next budget from member states that don't reform their pension systems, Politico reports, citing senior officials.
  • The European Union's executive arm is mulling recommending retirement savings policies to individual countries to buttress their pension systems, according to Politico.
  • The plans wouldn't set retirement ages or mandate monthly payouts to pensioners, but incentivize citizens to save for retirement and encourage companies to offer corporate pension plans to employees, Politico said.

Full story: https://tinyurl.com/bdsm5eep

Write to Ed Frankl at edward.frankl@wsj.com

data.title: Euro Area Inflation Rises Slightly Above ECB Target as Core Prices Accelerate
data.description:
Euro area consumer price inflation was confirmed at 2.2% year-on-year in September 2025, up from 2.0% over the previous three months and slightly above the ECB’s 2.0% mid-point target.

Services inflation edged higher to 3.2% from 3.1% in August, while energy costs declined only 0.4%, compared with a 2.0% drop previously.

Prices for food, alcohol, and tobacco rose more slowly at 3.0%, down from 3.2%, reflecting weaker unprocessed food inflation.

Non-energy industrial goods inflation remained steady at 0.8%.

Meanwhile, core inflation—which excludes energy, food, alcohol, and tobacco—rose to 2.4%, above the preliminary 2.3% estimate and reversing an over three-year low of 2.3% in August, signaling persistent underlying price pressures.

data.title: Starbucks investors urge company to restart union talks
data.description:
Key points:
  • Shareholders express concern over deteriorating labor relations
  • Over 650 unionized Starbucks stores in the U.S
  • Talks stalled since April last year, union strikes occurred

Long-term Starbucks shareholders have written to the company to resume talks with its workers union to discuss staffing, wages and other issues, a letter posted Thursday on the New York City Comptroller's website showed.

The letter, signed by Comptroller Brad Lander, Trillium Asset Management, the Shareholder Association for Research and Education and Pensions Investment Research Consultants, was addressed to Starbucks' board members Jorgen Vig Knudstorpand and Beth Ford and called on the company to reach a contract agreement with Starbucks Workers United.

"We are concerned that Starbucks' labor relations have significantly deteriorated, as reflected by over one hundred Unfair Labor Practice complaints filed since the beginning of the year, in-store actions, partner walkouts and protests over store closings, and even strikes," the letter said.

Starbucks did not respond to a request for comment outside regular business hours.

The union has been at odds with management for months. Talks between Starbucks and Starbucks Workers United, which represents more than 12,000 baristas, began in April last year but have since stalled.

In December, union members staged multi-day strikes across several U.S. cities during the peak holiday season.

"Over three years have passed since the first successful union election by Starbucks Workers United and yet no contract agreement has been reached," the letter said.

The Starbucks' store in Buffalo, New York was the first company-owned location to unionize in the U.S. in December 2021.

There are over 650 unionized Starbucks stores currently in the country, according to the SBWU's website.

The SBWU republished excerpts from the shareholder letter on its website, without commenting further.

Last month, Starbucks said it would shutter underperforming stores in North America, including its flagship unionized outlet in Seattle, as CEO Brian Niccol pushed ahead with a $1 billion restructuring aimed at reviving sales.

data.title: KRKA, TOVARNA ZDRAVIL D.D. - Acquisition of treasury shares
data.description:
POLISH FINANCIAL SUPERVISION AUTHORITY

UNI - EN REPORT No 44 / 2025

Date of issue: 2025-10-17

Short name of the issuer

KRKA

Subject

Acquisition of treasury shares

Official market - legal basis

Unofficial market - legal basis

Contents of the report:

Based on the provisions of the Rules of the Ljubljana Stock Exchange, d. d., Ljubljana and Article

2 of the Commission Delegated Regulation (EU) 2016/1052 Krka, d. d., Novo mesto announces:

With a reference to the public notice from 20 July 2023, Krka is publicly disclosing information on

transactions relating to buy-back programme in the period from 10 October 2025 up to and including

16 October 2025. Krka acquired 15,737 treasury shares in the total value of EUR 3,089,259 on the

Ljubljana Stock Exchange as follows:

- on 10 October 2025: 2,895 shares at a VWAP of EUR 182.47,

- on 13 October 2025: 3,219 shares at a VWAP of EUR 197.20,

- on 14 October 2025: 3,334 shares at a VWAP of EUR 197.53,

- on 15 October 2025: 3,083 shares at a VWAP of EUR 203.35 and

- on 16 October 2025: 3,206 shares at a VWAP of EUR 199.86.

Price is calculated as volume weighted average price (VWAP).

Ilirika d.d., Intercapital d.d. and NLB d.d. were brokers in these transactions. Detailed report on

acquisition of treasury shares attached.

The total number of treasury shares prior to these transactions was 2,349,715 representing 7.165%

of all shares issued.

The total number of treasury shares after these transactions is 2,365,452 representing 7.213% of

all shares issued.

The notice will be published on the Company's website (www.krka.biz) from 17 October 2025 onwards,

for a minimum period of 5 years.

Annexes

File Description

Additional attachments can be found below:

20251017_110905_0171842592_Detailed_report_on_acquisition_of_treasury_shares.pdf

data.title: Enersize announces outcome of warrant exercise of series TO4
data.description:
THIS PRESS RELEASE MAY NOT BE MADE PUBLIC, PUBLISHED OR DISTRIBUTED, DIRECTLY OR INDIRECTLY, IN OR INTO, AUSTRALIA, BELARUS, HONG KONG, JAPAN, CANADA, NEW ZEALAND, RUSSIA, SWITZERLAND, SINGAPORE, SOUTH AFRICA, SOUTH KOREA, THE UNITED STATES OR ANY OTHER JURISDICTION IN WHICH SUCH ACTIONS, WHOLLY OR IN PART, WOULD BE SUBJECT TO LEGAL RESTRICTIONS. THIS PRESS RELEASE DOES NOT CONSTITUTE AN OFFER TO BUY SECURITIES IN ENERSIZE OYJ. SEE SECTION "IMPORTANT INFORMATION" AT THE END OF THIS PRESS RELEASE.

Enersize Oyj (“Enersize” or the “Company”) announces the outcome of the exercise of warrants of series TO4, which were issued in connection with the Company's rights issue resolved by the extraordinary general meeting on 9 May 2025. In total, 162,012,967 warrants series TO4 were exercised for subscription of 162,012,967 shares, meaning that approximately 3.9 per cent of all outstanding warrants of series TO4 were exercised for subscription of shares. Through the exercise of warrants of series TO4, the Company will receive proceeds of approximately SEK 0.4 million before transaction costs.

The subscription period run from 1 October 2025 up to and including 15 October 2025. One (1) warrant of series TO4 entitled the holder to subscribe for one (1) new share at a subscription price per share of SEK 0.0025 (EUR 0.000225 in Finland). In total, 162,012,967 warrants series TO4 were exercised for subscription of 162,012,967 shares, meaning that approximately 3.9 per cent of all outstanding warrants of series TO4 were exercised for subscription of shares. Through the exercise of warrants of series TO4, the Company will receive approximately SEK 0.4 million before transaction costs.

Number of shares and dilution

Through the exercise of the warrants of series TO4, the number of shares in Enersize increase by 162,012,967 shares to a total of 8,155,257,610 shares, resulting in a dilution effect of approximately 2.0 per cent.

Bergs Securities is the issuing agent in connection with the exercise of the warrants of series TO4 in Sweden, while Nordea Bank Abp is acting as the issuing agent in Finland.

IMPORTANT INFORMATION

Publication, release or distribution of this press release may in certain jurisdictions be subject to legal restrictions and persons in the jurisdictions where this press release has been made public or distributed should be informed of and follow such legal restrictions. The recipient of this press release is responsible for using this press release and the information herein in accordance with applicable rules in each jurisdiction. This press release does not constitute an offer or solicitation to buy or subscribe for any securities in Enersize in any jurisdiction, either from Enersize or from anyone else.

This press release is not a prospectus according to the definition in Regulation (EU) 2017/1129 (the “Prospectus Regulation”) or in any other applicable securities markets laws and has not been approved by any regulatory authority in any jurisdiction. Furthermore, this press release does not constitute a document in the form prescribed by Annex IX of the Prospectus Regulation.

This press release does not identify or purport to identify any risks (direct or indirect) that may be associated with an investment in new shares. The information contained in this press release is for the sole purpose of describing the background to the financing and does not purport to be complete or exhaustive.

This press release does not constitute an offer or solicitation to buy or subscribe for securities in the United States. The securities mentioned herein may not be sold in the United States without registration, or without an exemption from registration, under the U.S. Securities Act from 1933 (“Securities Act”), and may not be offered or sold within the United States without being registered, covered by an exemption from, or part of a transaction that is not subject to the registration requirements according to the Securities Act. There is no intention to register any securities mentioned herein in the United States or to issue a public offering of such securities in the United States. data.title: EssilorLuxottica Target Raised to EUR342 From EUR284 by Bank of America
EssilorLuxottica Raised to Buy From Neutral by Bank of America
Buy Infosys; target of Rs 1650: Emkay Global Financial
data.description:
Emkay Global Financial' research report on Infosys

Infosys delivered an in-line operating performance in Q2. Revenue grew 2.2% CC QoQ, aided by ~20bps contribution from M&As and improved realizations from Project Maximus amid soft volumes. EBITM expanded by 20bps QoQ to 21.0%, coming in slightly lower than our estimate. Large deal TCV in Q2 was strong at USD3.1bn, of which 67% is net new. In addition, Infosys signed a USD1.6bn mega-deal with NHS (100% net new) after the end of Q2; the deal will start ramping up within the current fiscal year. The company has narrowed its FY26 revenue growth guidance to 2-3% CC (earlier 1-3%), implying CQGR of -0.2% to -1.5% in H2. H2 is expected to be seasonally softer due to fewer working days, furloughs, and calendar effects, and the revised guidance incorporates elevated uncertainty at the lower end and a stable environment at the upper end. The guidance does not include any revenue from the Telstra JV (expected to close in H2; Versent reported FY25 revenue of AUD211.4mn). It has retained EBITM guidance range of 20-22% for FY26. We largely retain FY26-28E EPS, factoring in an in-line Q2.

Outlook

We retain BUY on the stock with TP of Rs1,650 at 21x Sep-27E EPS.

For all recommendations report, click here

data.title: Continental Shares Climb After Strong Quarter, Outlook Confirmation
data.description:
By Maitane Sardon

Shares in Continental rose after the company posted third-quarter results ahead of expectations and confirmed its outlook for the full year.

The German car-parts and tire maker said sales came in at about 5 billion euros ($5.84 billion) in the third quarter, according to preliminary results. The figure beat consensus estimates of 4.9 billion euros, it said.

Shares were 7.4% higher at 58.72 euros in European morning trade Friday.

Continental, which is set to publish third-quarter figures on Nov. 6, said it expects the adjusted earnings before interest and taxes margin to be around 11.4% in the quarter, above a consensus forecast of 9.5%.

The tires business had a strong start to the winter, with preliminary sales of around 3.5 billion euros and an EBIT margin of around 14.3%, Continental said. The results almost completely offset the negative effects of lower volumes, exchange rates and U.S. tariffs, it said.

The strong results are due in part to a strong price/mix, with the solid momentum expected to continue into the fourth quarter, Deutsche Bank analyst Christoph Laskawi said. The beat is also positive for tire maker Pirelli, Laskawi added.

Continental's update came days after French peer Michelin issued a profit warning, citing worsening market conditions and a sharp decline in North America sales. Sales volumes in its North America business fell around 10% in the third quarter, Michelin said.

Continental and Michelin have a similar exposure to the Americas, which means Michelin's issues are likely internal rather than due to market conditions, and internal issues are more easily fixed, Jefferies analysts said in a research note.

Continental said it continues to expect group sales between 19.5 billion euros and 21 billion euros for the year, and an adjusted earnings before interest and taxes margin of between 10% and 11%.

Write to Maitane Sardon at maitane.sardon@wsj.com

data.title: Continental Results Show It Can Keep Profitability Even Under Tough Conditions — Market Talk
data.description: Continental's third-quarter performance shows the brand is strong enough to support profitability even under pressure from tariffs or high raw-material costs, Jefferies analysts say in a research note. The same applies to Italian peer Pirelli, the analysts say. Continental's performance is commendable and the company beat forecasts clearly, specially in its tires division. The better-than-expected print and the unchanged full-year guidance make the fourth-quarter targets easy to achieve, Jefferies adds. Shares are up 8% at 59.04 euros.(maitane.sardon@wsj.com)

data.title: Barstool Sports founder Dave Portnoy buys record-setting $28 million waterfront estate in Florida Keys
data.description:
By Kelsi Karruli

The 10,228-square-foot property has two swimming pools plus its own private beach and boat dock.

Dave Portnoy at an NBA Finals game between the Dallas Mavericks and the Boston Celtics in Dallas in 2024.

Barstool Sports president Dave Portnoy has reportedly found himself a new "sanctuary" in the Florida Keys, where he is understood to have just purchased a staggering compound for $27.75 million-breaking the record for the highest property sale in the picturesque village of Islamorada.

The 48-year-old, who already owned more than $95 million worth of real estate before his latest jaw-dropping buy, just closed on the eight-bedroom, 9.5-bathroom abode on Monday, according to the Wall Street Journal.

Despite the record-breaking sum Portnoy handed over for the home, he did manage to negotiate a lower price with the seller, who had originally listed the megamansion for $31.2 million in June.

Portnoy's newest abode sits on a 1.65-acre waterfront lot and spans 10,228 square feet.

This "rare offering in the heart of the Florida Keys" features "150 feet of private waterfront, dockage, boat ramp, private beach, two pools, and lush, mature tropical landscaping," the listing states.

The village of Islamorada in the Florida Keys.

The sale set a record for Islamorada, a village that is made up of a string of six islands between the Atlantic and the Gulf of Mexico. The previous record of $22.65 million was set in 2023.

Listing agent Leslie Leopold, of Ocean Sotheby's International Realty, told the Wall Street Journal that the luxury market in Islamorada has seen a recent boost thanks to a spate of newly constructed homes hitting the market.

Portnoy's new residence is among those new builds causing a pricey stir in the local area, having been completed in 2024-five years after a former property that occupied the parcel was sold for just $2.3 million.

Today, the mansion that stands in its place offers an extraordinary level of luxury, from its gleaming white interior to its perfectly landscaped gardens.

The main residence measures 7,562 square feet and features five bedrooms, six bathrooms, an office, and several lavish amenities.

The expansive living area boasts a stunning 180-bottle wine cellar and chic overhead lighting that accentuates the open floor plan. It also opens up to one of the many outdoor seating areas, offering sweeping views of the water and the Sunshine State's sunsets.

Elsewhere, there is a handmade wood bar with a wood sink, a Sonos sound system, and a media room with blackout shades-making it the perfect place to entertain.

Moving into the chef's kitchen, there are marble and dolomite countertops, a custom mosaic tile backsplash, a wine tap, and state-of-the-art appliances.

The primary suite is spacious and features large windows that flood the room with light. The elegant bathroom has a marble vanity and a large soaking tub.

There is also a large walk-in closet for the Barstool mogul to put all of his and his dogs' clothes.

Each chic bathroom comes complete with a Toto toilet, which can range in price from $1,595 to $2,266.

The dining area has built-in wall shelves, modern overhead lighting, and room for a large table.

Nearly every area of the home boasts access to the exquisite covered porches, which offer spectacular water views. One of the covered porches boasts an outdoor kitchen complete with a brick pizza oven, flat grill, and smoker.

There is also a tackle room, two laundry rooms, and a four-car garage.

Elsewhere on the grounds, there is a 2,666-square-foot guesthouse with three bedrooms, four bathrooms, a private pool, a chef's kitchen, and a private gated drive.

"With two gated driveways, serene lawns, and a high-end tropical ambiance, this estate seamlessly blends privacy, prestige, and paradise. A one-of-a-kind legacy property - experience the ultimate Florida Keys lifestyle," the listing adds.

Realtor.com has reached out to Portnoy for comment.

Portnoy owns around $95 million worth of real estate in Massachusetts, Florida, and New York.

In 2021, Portnoy splashed out $13.98 million for a waterfront mansion in Miami, the Real Deal reported. The gated property had been on and off the market for years, both as a rental and for sale. It was priced at nearly $15 million when Portnoy picked it up at a discount.

Three years later, the home has an estimated value of $17.7 million, according to Realtor.com.

The two-story contemporary features nine bedrooms and nine baths. It comes with a one-bedroom, one-bath guesthouse.

Built in 1939, the 6,000-square-foot home has since been restored and renovated. The lushly landscaped acreage includes a pool, patio, beach cabana, and private dock.

The living room features a wood-burning fireplace, goat skin walls, and three sets of French doors that open to a covered outdoor seating area.

More from Realtor.com: I'm 47, and I Cashed Out My IRA to Buy a Tiny Home and 5 Acres for $133K

There's also a formal dining area, a kitchen with a marble center island, and a library. Upstairs, the primary suite comes with a private balcony.

Although lavish, that home has since been plagued with mold issues, according to a post shared on Portnoy's X account, where he asked his followers in August 2024 to recommend "the best mold company in the history of Miami" to come and look at his "moldy ass house."

Luckily for the Barstool Sports founder, his Miami abode is not the only place he has to bed down-nor is Florida the only state where he has racked up property.

The Massachusetts native, who grew up in the North Shore town of Swampscott, picked up two adjacent homes in Nantucket for $42 million in 2023.

According to multiple reports, the purchase set a record for the island and the state at the time. He reportedly spent an additional $2 million on furniture.

More from Realtor.com: Julia Roberts Reveals Cast of New Movie 'After the Hunt' Lived With Her for 'Weeks' While Husband Danny Moder Served as their Chef

The price was more than worth it to secure a home in his "favorite place on Earth," he said.

"Nantucket has always been my favorite place on Earth," he wrote in a post on X. "The best day of my year was heading to the island for vacation. The worst day was taking the ferry back home."

The two homes sit on 1.2 acres on the popular vacation isle: a 5,200-square-foot main residence and a two-bedroom guesthouse. There's also a pool and harbor views for miles.

Perhaps the most intriguing feature of the home is its underground tunnel, which connects the guesthouse with a separate studio, both of which are separate from the enormous main residence.

According to the Wall Street Journal, the property was designed by Andrew Kotchen of Workshop/APD and completed in 2018.

More from Realtor.com: Halloween Auction of Real-Life 'Conjuring' Home Is Canceled-Marking Latest Twist in Property's Eerie History

Real estate agent Shellie Dunlap, who represented the sellers in the deal, would not confirm that Portnoy was the buyer of the property. However, she did reveal that the main home boasts four bedrooms, including a primary suite with an indoor-outdoor shower and glass doors that open up to the harbor.

Although Barstool Sports was founded in Massachusetts, it's now a media behemoth in New York City, where it has offices in the trendy borough of Brooklyn.

More from Realtor.com: Palisades Fire Suspect Jonathan Rinderknecht 'Threatened to Burn Down His Sister's House' Before Arrest for Blaze That Killed 12

When the media mogul wants some R&R closer to the office, he can head to his Montauk mansion, which he is understood to have purchased for $9.8 million in 2021.

The 5,700-square-foot vacation getaway in the trendy Hamptons enclave has five bedrooms, a pool, and endless ocean views.

Although reports surfaced that the cash-strapped owner was looking to sell the property for $14 million, he denied that he had put his Hamptons home on the market.

This story originally ran on Realtor.com.

This content was created by MarketWatch, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. MarketWatch is published independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal.

data.title: Sterling Falls as Risk Aversion, Fiscal Worries Weigh — Market Talk
data.description: Sterling falls as widespread risk aversion and U.K. fiscal concerns weigh. Loan losses at U.S. regional banks dampen risk appetite along with U.S.-China tensions and an ongoing U.S. government shutdown. In the U.K., Treasury chief Rachel Reeves announced plans to target wealthy taxpayers ahead of the November 26 autumn budget. Reeves faces an "uphill battle" to plug the fiscal gap and reassure markets she has a credible plan for growth, Ebury's Matthew Ryan says in a note. Tax increases and a modest rise in borrowing look likely but investors will be "clamouring for commitments to at least mild spending cuts." Sterling falls 0.1% to $1.3423. The euro rises 0.2% to a one-week high of 0.8724 pounds, LSEG data show. (renae.dyer@wsj.com)

data.title: Pearson's sales growth accelerates on virtual learning and English test demand
data.description:
British education company Pearson said its underlying sales growth accelerated to 4% in the third quarter, up from 2% in the first half, driven by demand for virtual learning, where enrolments rose 13% for the 2025/26 academic year.

Chief Executive Omar Abbosh said the performance set the company up for a "strong Q4".

As well as strength in virtual learning and assessment, and qualifications, he pointed to a return to growth in English language learning in the quarter.

"Earlier in the year there was some worry about the Pearson Test of English (PTE) due to the various migration policies that you're seeing around the world," he told Reuters on Friday.

"And actually the PTE performed extremely well in the context."

Shares in the company rose 3.6% after it said it expected t to meet market expectations for the year.

Analysts have forecast the group will report underlying sales growth of 4% and adjusted operating profit of about 606 million pounds ($813 million), based on the dollar exchange rate at the end of September.

($1 = 0.7451 pounds)

data.title: Banks, defense tumble, Essilorluxottica shines
data.description:
Banks, defense tumble, Essilorluxottica shines

European shares, down 1.7%, are on course for their biggest daily drop since August 1, as renewed concerns about the health of U.S. regional banks hurts the stock of lenders around the world.

The pan-European STOXX 600 index was set to wipe out weekly gains, with banks (.SX7P) down 3.1% and defence stocks (.SXPARO) down 3.5%.

The U.S. regional banks index dropped 6.3% on Thursday after announcements from two lenders fuelled investor unease about hidden credit stress.

Read more about the global stocks rout here.

Shares in European arms makers fell with news of a planned next summit between U.S President Donald Trump and with Russian President Vladimir Putin tampering YTD gains.

Beyond banks, shares in Ray-Ban maker EssilorLuxottica hit an all-time high, surging over 11% after the eyewear giant's record quarter showed sales of its Ray-Ban Meta smart glasses gaining momentum.

In corporate updates, Spain's BBVA shares jumped 7% after it failed to convince Sabadell shareholders to back its 16.32 billion euro ($19.07 billion) hostile takeover bid. Sabadell shares fell 7%.

(Joice Alves)

data.title: Surge in wind output to weigh on Monday spot prices
data.description:
A strong rise in regional wind power supply is expected to bring spot prices down on Monday.

The German and French Monday baseload power contracts (TRDEBD3), (TRFRBD3) had not started trading by 0824 GMT on Friday, after closing at respectively 108 and 90 euros per megawatt hour on Thursday, LSEG data showed.

German residual load is seen decreasing by 16.2 gigawatts (GW) on Monday, with the rest of the region also recording losses, mainly due to an increase in wind power supply, said LSEG analyst Xiulan He.

German wind power output is expected to jump by 16.9 GW to 32.6 GW on Monday, while French wind power generation is projected to add 8 GW to 12.6 GW, LSEG data showed.

German solar power supply is forecast to dip by 2.3 GW to 3.7 GW.

Power consumption in Germany is forecast to fall 1.5 GW to 55.4 GW on Monday, while demand in France is expected to drop by 2.5 GW to 45.1 GW.

French nuclear availability rose three percentage points to 72% of total capacity.

German year-ahead power (TRDEBYZ6) shed 0.7% to 87.40 euros/MWh, while the French 2026 baseload contract (TRFRBYZ6) fell 0.2% to 56.80 euros/MWh.

In the European carbon market (CFI2Zc1), the benchmark contract fell 1.6% to 78.28 euros a metric ton.

The EEX bourse said it will extend maturities for its German Power Base Month Futures and Options contracts to 24 months on December 8, to allow for extended trading opportunities.

In a statement announcing the date, it said that with close to 5,000 TWh traded so far this year of German power derivatives, the country's contribution makes it the EEX's most liquid power futures market segment.

Tomra misses estimates after delayed deposit return scheme roll-out
data.description:
By Vera Dvorakova and Agnieszka Olenska

Norwegian recycling technology provider Tomra reported lower-than-expected earnings on Friday, citing a delayed deposit return vending machine roll-out in Poland and weakness in its recycling division.

WHY IT MATTERS

Tomra Systems' collection division provides vending machines for deposit and return schemes which are based on collecting and recycling cans and bottles.

Several countries across Europe, Tomra's largest market, have been introducing deposit and return schemes. Poland's scheme launched on October 1 and Portugal's is expected to kick in during 2026.

CONTEXT

Tomra flagged in July that the growth prospects for its collection division depend on developments in upcoming deposit markets in Poland and Portugal.

Poland will be the company's second largest market to date, the press release said.

BY THE NUMBERS

The Oslo-listed company posted adjusted earnings before interest, taxes, and amortization (EBITA) of 30 million euros ($35.14 million) for the quarter, below the 40 million euros expected by analysts in a poll compiled by the company.

Tomra has a strategic ambition to have 5% annual growth in existing markets, CEO Tove Andersen said in a conference call.

Tomra's shares opened 12.6% lower on Friday.

KEY QUOTES

"There needs to be a significant ramp up, which means that there will be a very hectic period starting now. And we think this will really last until mid next year," CEO Tove Andersen told Reuters.

She added that the European market for reverse vending machines will at least double from now until 2029 or 2030, enabling Tomra to meet its growth targets.

($1 = 0.8538 euros)
